steps.title: Identify the core disagreement
steps.content: Fowler argues mandatory voting makes results “better reflect the preferences of the country as a whole.” Singh and Roy contradict this: forced voters spend less time researching, so “votes from these voters may not reflect their actual preferences.”

steps.title: Determine which answer reflects Singh and Roy's response
steps.content: Choice C directly states that mandatory voting “does not necessarily lead to election outcomes that better represent the preferences of the country as a whole” — the opposite of what Fowler claims. The other three choices propose consequences not discussed in the texts: population-size thresholds (A), increased civic engagement (B), or easier voting procedures (D). That matches choice C — C is correct.
